subject: Re: WarpTools create_settings --folder_data problem
body:
Thank you, Alister! That's much clearer for me to understand how warp 
works! 

在2025年3月27日星期四 UTC+8 15:45:33<alist...@gmail.com> 写道：

> Hi Yoko,
>
> This has come up before and it’s a bit different from how warp desktop 
> worked: 
> - defocus handedness for a tilt series can be in one of two states 
> referred to as “flip” and “no-flip”
> - ts_defocus_hand reports what this state should be set to for a dataset
> - it is not expected that changing the state would change the outcome of 
> the defocus handedness check, the check is telling you an absolute state 
> that you should set to for your dataset 
>
> To confirm whether the flag has been set you can check in your tilt series 
> xml files but if the program ran to completion I wouldn’t worry!
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> c.f https://github.com/warpem/warp/issues/145#issuecomment-2168057181
>
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Mar 26, 2025, at 22:02, yoko “yokomaz” maz <chenxia...@gmail.com> 
> wrote:
>
> ﻿ Thank you, Alister! I've solved this problem and continued my learning 
> along the "Quick Start: Tilt Series" guide. Since I started with .tif 
> files, I used ts_stack to create a .st file and used AreTomo2 to estimate 
> alignment files. Then, I imported the alignment files (.xf and .tlt) into 
> warp_tiltseries.settings using ts_import_alignment. Now, I have reached 
> the step of checking the handedness of defocus. When I ran ts_defocus_hand 
> --check, I got a value of -0.954. To set the correct handedness, I used 
> --set_flip, but after rerunning ts_defocus_hand, it still shows -0.954. I 
> suspect that ts_defocus_hand --set_flip didn't work because in the 
> output, there is no message saying "Finished processing in xx:xx:xx", only 
> with "Setting defocus handedness to 'flip' for all tilt series...    
> 10/10", is that means that filp has been set?
>
> 在2025年3月18日星期二 UTC+8 21:50:01<alist...@gmail.com> 写道：
>
>> Hey! In your processing directory there should be a logs folder, check in 
>> there for logs which should tell you why things have failed
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>>
>> On Mar 17, 2025, at 23:53, yoko “yokomaz” maz <chenxia...@gmail.com> 
>> wrote:
>>
>> ﻿
>> Hey there! 
>>
>> I'm new to cryo-em and I'm now following the tutorial <Quick Start: Tilt 
>> Series 
>> From the tutorial>, when I used 
>>
>> "WarpTools create_settings --folder_data ./frames --folder_processing 
>>  --output warp_frameseries.settings --extension "*.tif" --angpix 3.3702 
>> --exposure 2.35" 
>>
>> to create a .settings file, and I continued with 
>>
>> "WarpTools fs_motion_and_ctf --settings warp_frameseries.settings 
>> --out_averages"
>>
>>  the programe was looking for data at warp_frameseries/*.tif. The error 
>> is 
>>
>> Failed to process 
>> xx/xx/xx/DEF_TOMO2/warp_frameseries/TS_034_040_-40.0.tif, marked as 
>> unselected
>>
>> As far as I understand, --folder_data is the location where we have *.tif 
>> and --folder_processing is the location where we store the result of the 
>> processing. Can anybody help me this? Thanks a lot!
>>
>> The organization of my file system is:
>> xx/xx/xx/DEF_TOMO2/warp_frameseries.settings
>> xx/xx/xx/DEF_TOMO2/frames/*.tif
>> xx/xx/xx/DEF_TOMO2/metadata/mdocs_modified/*.mdoc
>> xx/xx/xx/DEF_TOMO2/warp_frameseries
>>
>> --
